name: Will adding JSON-LD schema to every page slow down my site or hurt performance?

mainEntity.name: Will adding JSON-LD schema to every page slow down my site or hurt performance?

acceptedAnswer.text:
JSON-LD markup itself has minimal performance cost; the real risks come from bloated scripts, unnecessary duplication, or heavy client-side injection. A few kilobytes of schema in `<head>` per page is typically negligible.

However, badly implemented schema can complicate HTML, debugging, and caching.

Key factors:
- **Size:** Keep JSON-LD concise—no massive embedded data dumps.
- **Placement:** Prefer static markup over runtime JavaScript injection.
- **Duplication:** Avoid multiple overlapping schema blocks on the same page.
- **Caching:** For static sites, templates with schema compress well and are cache-friendly.

Measure page size before and after implementation, but in most cases, per-page JSON-LD will not materially impact load times.

Focus on correctness and simplicity rather than premature micro-optimizing of the schema payload.
acceptedAnswer.description: Per-page JSON-LD is usually just a small script block and has negligible performance impact if you keep it concise, static, and free of duplication. Performance issues arise more from heavy client-side injection or oversized data blobs than from normal schema markup in the `<head>`.

description: Per-page JSON-LD is usually just a small script block and has negligible performance impact if you keep it concise, static, and free of duplication. Performance
